linting.notes: All linters configured in pyproject.toml and setup.cfg. Black uses line length 88. Mypy configured with pydantic plugin. Makefile provides 'make lint' target that runs all three linters plus runtime checks.

testing.run_command: poetry run python -m pytest tests/ -n auto
testing.mock_patterns: Uses pytest-mock and pytest-asyncio for async testing. Docker containers used for integration tests.
testing.notes: Tests use pytest with asyncio mode auto. Tests are split into multiple categories run separately due to flakiness: metrics, kafka, parallel, grpc, env, cli. The odh-mlserver tox environment is the primary test target (excludes cli tests). Tests require Docker for some integration scenarios. Supports parallel execution with pytest-xdist (-n auto).

gating_checks.name: generate
gating_checks.command: make generate-dataplane && make generate-model-repository && make lint-no-changes

ci.notes: CI runs on Python 3.9, 3.10, 3.11, 3.12 across Ubuntu 24.04 (and MacOS on merge). Poetry version 2.1.1 is pinned. Tekton pipelines handle container builds with multi-arch support for OpenShift deployments. Tests are split into separate jobs (mlserver core, individual runtimes, all-runtimes integration).

build.notes: Uses Poetry 2.1.1 for dependency management. Lock files are managed with 'make lock'. Container builds use multi-stage Dockerfile with UBI9 minimal base. Supports multiple ML runtimes (lightgbm, onnx, sklearn, xgboost) installed as separate packages. Python 3.12 is the default for containers.

conventions.test_naming: Functions named test_* or Test* classes
conventions.import_style: Absolute imports preferred, uses importlib import mode
conventions.notes: Tests organized by feature (batching, codecs, grpc, kafka, metrics, parallel, repository, rest). Async tests use pytest-asyncio with auto mode. Some tests require Docker daemon access.

Passed: no type errors in 101 source files

container_recipe.notes: Container needs Docker socket mounted for integration tests. Poetry installs to virtual environment by default. Some tests (kafka, parallel, grpc) may be flaky when run in parallel with the full suite.
